title: feat: rebuild observability dashboards
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds five focused Grafana dashboards for platform usage, users and balances, BYOP apps, community models, and Core API.
- Preserves all 12 existing dashboards unchanged under `Legacy Observability` and provisions the rebuilt set under `Observability`.
- Adds the deployed `generation_usage_hourly` and `byop_app_daily` Tinybird aggregate definitions, plus the implementation plan.
- Uses 30-day defaults, bar-first visuals, shared query results, Paid/Quest terminology, and production-compatible datasource filters.

Checks:
- `npm run check`
- Biome check for the Worker entrypoint
- JSON validation for all 17 dashboards
- 28 direct Tinybird queries and 45-panel local UI audit

The PR is intentionally draft. Production Grafana rollout is performed separately through the operations deployment workflow; this PR must not be merged yet.

gist.headline: The hive gets better eyes
gist.blurb: Five new observability dashboards are ready to watch the garden grow, while all 12 old dashboards remain safely filed under Legacy Observability. Polli has traded guesswork for bar charts.
gist.summary: Rebuilt Grafana observability into five focused dashboards for platform usage, users and balances, BYOP apps, community models, and Core API, backed by new Tinybird generation_usage_hourly and byop_app_daily aggregates while preserving all 12 existing dashboards under Legacy Observability.
gist.impact: Operations contributors get 30-day, bar-first views with shared query results and production-compatible datasource filters, making platform usage and API health easier to inspect before the separate production rollout.
gist.visual_concept: Depict a cozy garden observatory: Polli, wearing tiny analyst glasses, studies five small CRT dashboard screens showing friendly bar charts for platform activity, users, BYOP apps, community models, and the Core API. Nearby, a neatly labeled Legacy Observability filing shelf holds twelve preserved scrolls, while code-plants feed data into the new screens.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polli, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and tiny analyst glasses, tending a lime green (#ecf874) garden observatory with five glowing CRT monitors displaying simple bar charts; preserved dashboard scrolls sit on a warm cream shelf labeled in pixel font 'LEGACY'. Code-plants and small flowers feed the screens, with soft pastel mint, lavender, and peach gradients, cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
